text.title: Golden Set Curation and Maintenance Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Golden set passes 100% on a broken model. Production bugs never trigger regressions.
antipattern.root-cause: All items tagged happy_path; no edge_case / adversarial / known_failure_class items.
antipattern.fix: Run coverage report; reject promotion if any required bucket below 15%.

antipattern.symptom: LLM judge over-credits outputs containing right tokens for wrong reason. Pass rate looks high but quality regresses silently.
antipattern.root-cause: Anti-output field empty or omitted across items.
antipattern.fix: Schema requires anti_output as non-empty array; pre-merge script rejects items missing it.

antipattern.symptom: Same bug class returns Q after Q. Postmortems repeat. Trust in eval erodes.
antipattern.root-cause: No process links incident postmortem to golden-set candidate creation.
antipattern.fix: Add post-mortem checklist item: 'create golden candidate'; track conversion ratio in monthly review.

antipattern.symptom: Golden expected_output reflects last-year business rules. Model that correctly follows current rules fails the eval.
antipattern.fix: Calendar 90-day audit; tag items as still_valid / schema_changed / retire; retire-count reported in dataset bump notes.
